representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT author_id FROM authors;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: List author id from authors.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT title, author_id FROM books;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show title, author id from books.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT location, department_id, department_name FROM departments;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Display departments.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT salary, joining_date FROM employees;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Return salary, joining date from employees.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT price, product_id, product_name FROM products;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: List products.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT project_id FROM projects;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show project id from projects.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT rows_returned, created_at FROM query_history;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Display rows returned, created at from query history.

representative_examples.executed_sql:
SELECT T1.author_id, T2.price 
FROM authors AS T1 INNER JOIN books AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT author_id, price FROM books;
representative_examples.generated_sql:
SELECT T1.author_id, T2.price 
FROM authors AS T1 INNER JOIN books AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id
representative_examples.natural_language_query: List author id, price from books.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT employee_id FROM employee_projects;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Display employee id from employee projects.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT price, book_id FROM books;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Return price, book id from books.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT project_id FROM employee_projects;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show project id from employee projects.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT hours_per_week FROM employee_projects WHERE hours_per_week >= 3;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show employee projects with hours per week below 3.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT price, product_id, product_name FROM products WHERE price >= 5;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show products with price no lower than 5.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT id, vendor_name, city FROM vendors WHERE rating >= 8;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show vendors with rating above 8.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT location, department_id, department_name FROM departments WHERE location IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(location) >= 12;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show departments where location has at least 12 characters.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT employee_id FROM employee_projects WHERE hours_per_week >= 12;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show employee projects with hours per week at least 12.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT price, product_id, product_name FROM products WHERE price >= 14;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show products with price above 14.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT vendor_name, city, rating FROM vendors WHERE rating >= 17;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show vendors with rating no lower than 17.

representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT author_id FROM authors WHERE author_name IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(author_name) >= 19;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show authors where author name has at least 19 characters.

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT * FROM employee_projects ORDER BY hours_per_week DESC LIMIT 8
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT hours_per_week FROM employee_projects ORDER BY hours_per_week DESC LIMIT 8;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT * FROM employee_projects ORDER BY hours_per_week DESC LIMIT 8
representative_examples.natural_language_query: List the first 8 employee projects ordered by hours per week descending.

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 10
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT price, product_id, product_name FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 10;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 10
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Return 10 products ranked by price descending.

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT * FROM employee_projects ORDER BY employee_id ASC LIMIT 17
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT employee_id FROM employee_projects ORDER BY employee_id ASC LIMIT 17;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT * FROM employee_projects ORDER BY employee_id ASC LIMIT 17
representative_examples.natural_language_query: List the first 17 employee projects ordered by employee id ascending.

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 19;
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT price, product_id, product_name FROM products ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 19;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 19;
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Return 19 products ranked by price ascending.

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT id, vendor_name, city FROM vendors LIMIT 6
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT id, vendor_name, city FROM vendors LIMIT 6 OFFSET 0;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT id, vendor_name, city FROM vendors LIMIT 6
representative_examples.natural_language_query: List the next 6 vendors records.

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT DISTINCT department_name FROM departments WHERE LENGTH(department_name) >= 3
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT department_name FROM departments WHERE department_name IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(department_name) >= 3 ORDER BY department_name ASC;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT department_name FROM departments WHERE LENGTH(department_name) >= 3
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Which different department name values exist for departments with department name has at least 3 characters?

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT DISTINCT role FROM employee_projects WHERE LENGTH(role) >= 4
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT role FROM employee_projects WHERE role IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(role) >= 4 ORDER BY role ASC;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT role FROM employee_projects WHERE LENGTH(role) >= 4
representative_examples.natural_language_query: List unique role values from employee projects where role has at least 4 characters.

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT DISTINCT email FROM employees WHERE LENGTH(email) >= 5
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT email FROM employees WHERE email IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(email) >= 5 ORDER BY email ASC;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT email FROM employees WHERE LENGTH(email) >= 5
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show distinct email in employees after filtering for email has at least 5 characters.

representative_examples.executed_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT product_name 
FROM products 
WHERE LENGTH(product_name) >= 6
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT product_name FROM products WHERE product_name IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(product_name) >= 6 ORDER BY product_name ASC;
representative_examples.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT product_name 
FROM products 
WHERE LENGTH(product_name) >= 6
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Which different product name values exist for products with product name has at least 6 characters?

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT DISTINCT generation_mode FROM query_history WHERE LENGTH(generation_mode) >= 8
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT generation_mode FROM query_history WHERE generation_mode IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(generation_mode) >= 8 ORDER BY generation_mode ASC;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT generation_mode FROM query_history WHERE LENGTH(generation_mode) >= 8
representative_examples.natural_language_query: Show distinct generation mode in query history after filtering for generation mode has at least 8 characters.

representative_examples.executed_sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM authors WHERE LENGTH(country) >= 10
representative_examples.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM authors WHERE country IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(country) >= 10 ORDER BY country ASC;
representative_examples.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM authors WHERE LENGTH(country) >= 10
representative_examples.natural_language_query: List unique country values from authors where country has at least 10 characters.
